Genre Decoded

A Spotify Data Story

Genres have personalities — but not as different as you’d think.

We analysed 28,000 songs across 6 genres using Spotify’s audio fingerprints. Here’s what we found.

Scroll to explore 
<!-- LEFT: scrolling prose steps -->
<div class="scrolly-steps" id="genre-steps">

  <div class="step is-active" data-step="intro">
    <div class="step-label">Section 1 · Genre Attributes</div>
    <h2>What makes a genre a genre?</h2>
    <p>
      Spotify measures every song on six dimensions: how danceable it is,
      how much energy it carries, how happy it sounds, how acoustic it feels,
      how fast it moves, and how speech-heavy it is.
    </p>
    <p style="margin-top:0.75rem">
      Together, these form a <strong>sonic fingerprint</strong>. The radar chart
      shows the median fingerprint for all six genres at once.
    </p>
  </div>

  <div class="step" data-step="latin-edm">
    <div class="step-label">Section 1 · Latin vs EDM</div>
    <h2>The happiest genre isn't EDM — it's Latin.</h2>
    <p>
      <strong>Latin</strong> scores highest on both <em>valence</em> (happiness)
      and <em>danceability</em>. EDM is the most energetic, but sits surprisingly
      low on valence — intense, not joyful.
    </p>
    <div class="takeaway">
      <strong>Try it:</strong> Click <em>latin</em> and <em>edm</em> in the legend
      to isolate them and compare their radar shapes.
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="step" data-step="rap">
    <div class="step-label">Section 1 · Rap</div>
    <h2>Rap has one feature that sets it apart from everything else.</h2>
    <p>
      Speechiness — the measure of how many words are in a track — spikes
      hard for rap. Every other genre clusters near zero.
      This is the single clearest genre separator in the entire dataset.
    </p>
    <div class="takeaway">
      <strong>Try it:</strong> Click <em>Speechiness</em> in the feature buttons
      to highlight it across all genres in the bar chart.
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="step" data-step="pop">
    <div class="step-label">Section 1 · Pop</div>
    <h2>Pop is the genre of averages.</h2>
    <p>
      On every feature, pop sits closest to the dataset mean. No defining spike,
      no extreme score. Pop's commercial logic is to borrow from everywhere
      and offend no one.
    </p>
  </div>

  <div class="step" data-step="similarity">
    <div class="step-label">Section 1 · Takeaway</div>
    <h2>No genre is truly extreme. The gaps are smaller than you'd expect.</h2>
    <p>
      The radar polygons overlap significantly. Latin and R&amp;B are the most
      similar pair. EDM and R&amp;B are the most different — yet even they share
      significant overlap in emotional space.
    </p>
    <div class="takeaway">
      <strong>Next:</strong> If genres are already converging in sound,
      how much further have they come over the decades?
    </div>
  </div>

</div><!-- /scrolly-steps -->

<!-- RIGHT: sticky panel — OJS renders everything inside here -->
<div class="scrolly-sticky">
  <div class="chart-panel" id="main-chart-panel">

    <!--
      KEY ARCHITECTURE NOTE:
      We do NOT put empty divs here for OJS to find.
      Instead each OJS cell below outputs its DOM node directly,
      and Quarto injects it in document order — which is AFTER this HTML.

      The chart-panel grid layout applies to whatever children end up here,
      but the children are created by the OJS blocks below via ojs-output divs
      that Quarto inserts automatically.

      We use a wrapper div with a known id so we can appendChild into it
      AFTER the DOM is ready, using a MutationObserver approach — OR we
      simply let OJS return html`` nodes and Quarto places them in-flow.

      The cleanest approach: return html`` from each OJS cell, let Quarto
      place them. We move them into chart-panel via JS after render.
    -->

    <!-- Placeholder slots — filled by moveCharts() below -->
    <div id="slot-legend"></div>
    <div id="slot-filters"></div>
    <div id="slot-radar"></div>
    <div id="slot-bar"></div>
    <div id="slot-songs"></div>

  </div>
</div><!-- /scrolly-sticky -->
genreProfiles = FileAttachment("data/processed/genre_profiles.json").json()
topSongs      = FileAttachment("data/processed/top_songs.json").json()

// ── Constants ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FEATURES = ["Danceability", "Energy", "Valence", "Acousticness", "Tempo", "Speechiness"]
// ── Legend (interactive genre filter) ───────────────────────────────────────
//
// We use Inputs.checkbox from the stdlib — this is the idiomatic OJS pattern.
// It gives us a reactive Set automatically; no manual event dispatching needed.
//
// Note: we style it ourselves because we need custom pill appearance.

viewof activeGenres = {
  // Build a custom pill-based toggle, but return a proper OJS input
  // so other cells can depend on it reactively.

  const allGenres = genreProfiles.map(d => d.genre)
  const state = new Set(allGenres)   // all on initially

  const wrap = html`<div class="genre-legend"></div>`

  genreProfiles.forEach(d => {
    const item = wrap.appendChild(html`
      <div class="legend-item active" style="border-color: ${d.color}">
        <div class="legend-dot" style="background: ${d.color}"></div>
        <span>${d.genre.toUpperCase()}</span>
      </div>
    `)

    item.addEventListener("click", () => {
      if (state.has(d.genre)) {
        state.delete(d.genre)
        item.classList.remove("active")
        item.style.borderColor = "transparent"
        item.querySelector(".legend-dot").style.opacity = "0.3"
      } else {
        state.add(d.genre)
        item.classList.add("active")
        item.style.borderColor = d.color
        item.querySelector(".legend-dot").style.opacity = "1"
      }
      // Notify OJS of the change
      wrap.value = new Set(state)
      wrap.dispatchEvent(new Event("input", { bubbles: true }))
    })
  })

  wrap.value = state
  return wrap
}
// ── Feature filter buttons ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

viewof activeFeature = {
  const wrap = html`<div></div>`

  const label = wrap.appendChild(html`
    <div style="font-size:0.7rem;color:var(--text-dim);margin-bottom:0.4rem;
                letter-spacing:0.08em;text-transform:uppercase;">
      Highlight a feature →
    </div>
  `)

  const btnRow = wrap.appendChild(html`<div class="feature-filters"></div>`)

  wrap.value = null
  let activeBtn = null

  FEATURES.forEach(feat => {
    const btn = btnRow.appendChild(html`<button class="filter-btn">${feat}</button>`)
    btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
      if (activeBtn === btn) {
        btn.classList.remove("active")
        activeBtn = null
        wrap.value = null
      } else {
        if (activeBtn) activeBtn.classList.remove("active")
        btn.classList.add("active")
        activeBtn = btn
        wrap.value = feat
      }
      wrap.dispatchEvent(new Event("input", { bubbles: true }))
    })
  })

  return wrap
}
// ── Radar chart ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Returns an SVG element. OJS places it into the page automatically.
// Depends on: activeGenres (re-runs whenever the legend is clicked)

radarChart = {
  const W = 300
  const H = 300
  const cx = W / 2, cy = H / 2
  const R  = W * 0.35
  const N  = FEATURES.length
  const angles = FEATURES.map((_, i) => (Math.PI * 2 * i) / N - Math.PI / 2)

  // Helper: get [x, y] for a feature value on the radar
  function pt(featIndex, val) {
    const a = angles[featIndex]
    return [cx + val * R * Math.cos(a), cy + val * R * Math.sin(a)]
  }

  // Helper: polygon points string from array of [x,y] pairs
  function polyPoints(pairs) {
    return pairs.map(p => p.join(",")).join(" ")
  }

  const svgNS = "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
  const svg = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "svg")
  svg.setAttribute("viewBox", `0 0 ${W} ${H}`)
  svg.setAttribute("width", "100%")
  svg.style.overflow = "visible"
  svg.style.display = "block"

  // Grid rings
  ;[0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0].forEach(r => {
    const ring = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "polygon")
    ring.setAttribute("points", polyPoints(angles.map(a => [
      cx + r * R * Math.cos(a),
      cy + r * R * Math.sin(a)
    ])))
    ring.setAttribute("fill", "none")
    ring.setAttribute("stroke", "#2a2a2a")
    ring.setAttribute("stroke-width", "1")
    svg.appendChild(ring)
  })

  // Axis lines + labels
  FEATURES.forEach((feat, i) => {
    const a = angles[i]
    const [x2, y2] = [cx + R * Math.cos(a), cy + R * Math.sin(a)]

    const line = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "line")
    line.setAttribute("x1", cx); line.setAttribute("y1", cy)
    line.setAttribute("x2", x2); line.setAttribute("y2", y2)
    line.setAttribute("stroke", "#2a2a2a")
    line.setAttribute("stroke-width", "1")
    svg.appendChild(line)

    const lx = cx + (R + 22) * Math.cos(a)
    const ly = cy + (R + 22) * Math.sin(a)
    const isHighlighted = activeFeature === feat

    const txt = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "text")
    txt.setAttribute("x", lx)
    txt.setAttribute("y", ly)
    txt.setAttribute("text-anchor",
      Math.cos(a) > 0.1 ? "start" : Math.cos(a) < -0.1 ? "end" : "middle")
    txt.setAttribute("dominant-baseline",
      Math.sin(a) > 0.1 ? "hanging" : Math.sin(a) < -0.1 ? "auto" : "middle")
    txt.setAttribute("fill", isHighlighted ? "#f0f0f0" : "#555")
    txt.setAttribute("font-size", "9")
    txt.setAttribute("font-family", "DM Sans, sans-serif")
    txt.setAttribute("font-weight", isHighlighted ? "700" : "400")
    txt.textContent = feat.toUpperCase()
    svg.appendChild(txt)
  })

  // Genre polygons — dim genres that are toggled off
  genreProfiles.forEach(d => {
    const isOn = activeGenres.has(d.genre)
    const pairs = FEATURES.map((_, i) => pt(i, d.features[FEATURES[i]] ?? 0))

    const poly = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "polygon")
    poly.setAttribute("points", polyPoints(pairs))
    poly.setAttribute("fill", d.color)
    poly.setAttribute("fill-opacity", isOn ? "0.15" : "0.02")
    poly.setAttribute("stroke", d.color)
    poly.setAttribute("stroke-width", isOn ? "2.5" : "0.5")
    poly.setAttribute("stroke-opacity", isOn ? "0.9" : "0.15")
    poly.style.transition = "all 0.35s ease"
    svg.appendChild(poly)
  })

  // Wrap in a titled container
  const wrap = html`
    <div>
      <div style="font-size:0.7rem;color:var(--text-dim);margin-bottom:0.5rem;
                  letter-spacing:0.08em;text-transform:uppercase;">
        Radar — Audio Fingerprints
      </div>
      ${svg}
    </div>
  `
  return wrap
}
// ── Grouped bar chart ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Depends on: activeGenres, activeFeature

barChart = {
  const W = 300
  const H = 220
  const margin = { top: 10, right: 8, bottom: 36, left: 24 }
  const innerW = W - margin.left - margin.right
  const innerH = H - margin.top - margin.bottom

  const svgNS = "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
  const svg = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "svg")
  svg.setAttribute("viewBox", `0 0 ${W} ${H}`)
  svg.setAttribute("width", "100%")

  const g = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "g")
  g.setAttribute("transform", `translate(${margin.left},${margin.top})`)
  svg.appendChild(g)

  // Y-axis grid lines
  ;[0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1].forEach(v => {
    const y = innerH - v * innerH
    const gridLine = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "line")
    gridLine.setAttribute("x1", 0); gridLine.setAttribute("y1", y)
    gridLine.setAttribute("x2", innerW); gridLine.setAttribute("y2", y)
    gridLine.setAttribute("stroke", "#1e1e1e")
    gridLine.setAttribute("stroke-width", "1")
    g.appendChild(gridLine)

    const tick = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "text")
    tick.setAttribute("x", -4); tick.setAttribute("y", y)
    tick.setAttribute("text-anchor", "end")
    tick.setAttribute("dominant-baseline", "middle")
    tick.setAttribute("font-size", "7")
    tick.setAttribute("font-family", "DM Sans, sans-serif")
    tick.setAttribute("fill", "#444")
    tick.textContent = v.toFixed(2)
    g.appendChild(tick)
  })

  const featW   = innerW / FEATURES.length
  const barPad  = 1
  const genreW  = (featW - barPad * 2) / genreProfiles.length

  FEATURES.forEach((feat, fi) => {
    const isFeatHighlighted = activeFeature === feat || activeFeature === null

    // Feature label on x-axis
    const xlabel = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "text")
    xlabel.setAttribute("x", fi * featW + featW / 2)
    xlabel.setAttribute("y", innerH + 14)
    xlabel.setAttribute("text-anchor", "middle")
    xlabel.setAttribute("font-size", "7.5")
    xlabel.setAttribute("font-family", "DM Sans, sans-serif")
    xlabel.setAttribute("fill", activeFeature === feat ? "#f0f0f0" : "#444")
    xlabel.setAttribute("font-weight", activeFeature === feat ? "700" : "400")
    xlabel.textContent = feat.slice(0, 5).toUpperCase()
    g.appendChild(xlabel)

    genreProfiles.forEach((d, gi) => {
      const isGenreOn = activeGenres.has(d.genre)
      const val = d.features[feat] ?? 0
      const barH = val * innerH
      const x = fi * featW + barPad + gi * genreW
      const y = innerH - barH
      const opacity = (isFeatHighlighted && isGenreOn) ? 0.85 : 0.07

      const rect = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "rect")
      rect.setAttribute("x", x)
      rect.setAttribute("y", y)
      rect.setAttribute("width", Math.max(genreW - 1, 1))
      rect.setAttribute("height", barH)
      rect.setAttribute("fill", d.color)
      rect.setAttribute("opacity", opacity)
      rect.style.transition = "opacity 0.3s ease"

      // Tooltip on hover
      rect.addEventListener("mouseenter", e => {
        let tip = document.querySelector(".chart-tooltip")
        if (!tip) {
          tip = document.createElement("div")
          tip.className = "chart-tooltip"
          document.body.appendChild(tip)
        }
        tip.innerHTML = `<strong style="color:${d.color}">${d.genre.toUpperCase()}</strong><br>${feat}: ${val.toFixed(3)}`
        tip.classList.add("visible")
      })
      rect.addEventListener("mousemove", e => {
        const tip = document.querySelector(".chart-tooltip")
        if (tip) {
          tip.style.left = (e.clientX + 14) + "px"
          tip.style.top  = (e.clientY - 32) + "px"
        }
      })
      rect.addEventListener("mouseleave", () => {
        document.querySelector(".chart-tooltip")?.classList.remove("visible")
      })

      g.appendChild(rect)
    })
  })

  const wrap = html`
    <div>
      <div style="font-size:0.7rem;color:var(--text-dim);margin-bottom:0.5rem;
                  letter-spacing:0.08em;text-transform:uppercase;">
        Bar chart — Feature comparison
      </div>
      ${svg}
    </div>
  `
  return wrap
}
// ── Song preview cards ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Shows top 3 songs for the first solo-selected genre.
// If 0 or all genres are selected, shows nothing.

songCards = {
  const active = [...activeGenres]

  // Only show cards when exactly 1 genre is isolated
  if (active.length === 0 || active.length === genreProfiles.length) {
    return html`<div></div>`
  }

  const focusGenre = active[0]
  const color = genreProfiles.find(d => d.genre === focusGenre)?.color ?? "#888"
  const songs = topSongs.filter(s => s.genre === focusGenre).slice(0, 3)

  if (!songs.length) return html`<div></div>`

  return html`
    <div style="grid-column: 1 / -1; margin-top: 0.75rem;">
      <div style="font-size:0.7rem;color:var(--text-dim);margin-bottom:0.5rem;
                  letter-spacing:0.08em;text-transform:uppercase;">
        Top songs · ${focusGenre.toUpperCase()}
      </div>
      <div class="song-cards">
        ${songs.map((song, i) => html`
          <div class="song-card" style="border-color:${color}33">
            <div class="song-card-rank" style="color:${color}">${i + 1}</div>
            <div class="song-card-info">
              <div class="song-card-name">${song.track_name}</div>
              <div class="song-card-artist">${song.track_artist}</div>
            </div>
            <div class="song-card-pop">⬆ ${song.popularity}</div>
          </div>
        `)}
      </div>
    </div>
  `
}
// ── Move OJS output nodes into the chart-panel slots ────────────────────────
//
// OJS places each cell's output into a div[id^="ojs-cell-"] inside
// #quarto-content. We move those nodes into the pre-declared slot divs
// inside .chart-panel so the grid layout applies correctly.
//
// We do this once, after all cells have rendered, using requestAnimationFrame.

{
  function moveIntoSlots() {
    // Map: slot id → the OJS cell output element to move there
    // OJS cell outputs are identified by finding the rendered wrapper
    // that contains our specific root elements.

    function findCellContaining(selector) {
      // OJS wraps each cell in div.observablehq or div[id^="ojs-cell"]
      const el = document.querySelector(selector)
      if (!el) return null
      // Walk up to find the OJS cell wrapper
      let node = el
      while (node && node.parentElement) {
        if (
          node.parentElement.id === "quarto-content" ||
          node.parentElement.classList.contains("observablehq-center") ||
          node.parentElement.tagName === "MAIN"
        ) return node
        node = node.parentElement
      }
      return node
    }

    const slots = {
      "slot-legend":  document.querySelector(".genre-legend"),
      "slot-filters": document.querySelector(".feature-filters"),
      "slot-radar":   document.querySelector("[style*='Radar']"),
      "slot-bar":     document.querySelector("[style*='Bar chart']"),
    }

    // Simpler approach: just grab ALL ojs-cell outputs in order
    // and move them into chart-panel children sequentially
    const panel = document.getElementById("main-chart-panel")
    if (!panel) { requestAnimationFrame(moveIntoSlots); return }

    const slotLegend  = document.getElementById("slot-legend")
    const slotFilters = document.getElementById("slot-filters")
    const slotRadar   = document.getElementById("slot-radar")
    const slotBar     = document.getElementById("slot-bar")
    const slotSongs   = document.getElementById("slot-songs")

    // Find OJS outputs by their rendered class .observablehq
    const ojsCells = [...document.querySelectorAll(".observablehq")]
    if (ojsCells.length < 4) { requestAnimationFrame(moveIntoSlots); return }

    // The cells render in document order:
    // cell 0 = legend (viewof activeGenres)
    // cell 1 = filters (viewof activeFeature)
    // cell 2 = radarChart
    // cell 3 = barChart
    // cell 4 = songCards

    const legendEl  = document.querySelector(".genre-legend")?.closest(".observablehq")
    const filterEl  = document.querySelector(".feature-filters")?.closest(".observablehq")
    const radarEl   = ojsCells.find(c => c.querySelector("svg") && c !== filterEl)
    const barEl     = ojsCells.filter(c => c.querySelector("svg") && c !== filterEl)[1]
    const songEl    = document.querySelector(".song-cards")?.closest(".observablehq")

    if (legendEl  && slotLegend)  slotLegend.appendChild(legendEl)
    if (filterEl  && slotFilters) slotFilters.appendChild(filterEl)
    if (radarEl   && slotRadar)   slotRadar.appendChild(radarEl)
    if (barEl     && slotBar)     slotBar.appendChild(barEl)
    if (songEl    && slotSongs)   slotSongs.appendChild(songEl)
  }

  // Give OJS time to render all cells first
  setTimeout(moveIntoSlots, 300)
}
